obligations.reason: The scope exposes external JTAG pins, debug-mode signals, DMI/JTAG instances, jtag_hash/jtag_unlock signals, and debug reset/request outputs. External debug control is security-sensitive and should be mediated before affecting hart/debug state.
obligations.subject: external JTAG/DTM interface
obligations.operation: trigger debug request, issue DMI read/write, assert non-debug-module reset, activate debug module
obligations.object: debug capability: debug_req_o, ndmreset_o, dmactive_o, DMI/debug module registers or bus-facing debug master/slave signals
obligations.expected_guard: debug authentication/authorization such as jtag_unlock, lifecycle/testmode gating, or explicit debug enable before external JTAG can issue DMI operations or reset/halt harts
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/DTM inputs tck_i/tms_i/trst_ni/td_i -> dmi_jtag/SimJTAG -> DMI/debug request signals -> debug module outputs debug_req_o/ndmreset_o/dmactive_o and any debug master/slave bus path
required_evidence_slots: guard expression or absence at use site
knowledge_used: Debug/JTAG/test access paths may bypass normal access control; check protected object reachability and authorization guards.
Evidence closure needs subject, operation, object, expected guard, observed behavior, path/reachability, and source locations.
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer shows relevant ports/signals/instances but not full debug connection logic; inspect visible RTL around lines 233-351 and debug-module instantiation/assignments.

obligations.reason: reglk_wrapper exposes a software-visible AXI interface and outputs lock-control bits that likely mediate protected peripheral registers; it also has jtag_unlock and acct_ctrl_i inputs. Need to verify that lock policy cannot be modified or cleared by an unauthorized AXI requester or unauthenticated JTAG unlock.
obligations.subject: AXI software-visible requester and JTAG unlock input
obligations.operation: write or clear register-lock policy bits
obligations.expected_guard: writes to lock/control registers should be permitted only under authorized conditions such as existing lock bits allowing update, privileged/account-control mediation, and any JTAG unlock path should itself be authenticated
obligations.candidate_path: reglk_wrapper AXI write path ariane_axi -> axi_lite_interface decoded en/we/address/wdata -> reglk_ctrl register -> reglk_ctrl_o -> reglk_ctrl in riscv_peripherals and downstream wrappers
state_conditions_to_check: whether bits are sticky after set
required_evidence_slots: subject AXI requester and/or JTAG unlock source
knowledge_used: Lock/sticky bit/write protection must protect all write paths and should not be clearable after set unless authorized.
CSR/MMIO access control requires requester, address decode, read/write enable, protected register, and expected guard.
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer identifies ports and internal signals but not the always-block write conditions; inspect lines around 38-130.

obligations.reason: acc_ctrl is used in riscv_peripherals to gate at least boot ROM access by privilege level, and acct_wrapper has AXI write inputs plus reglk_ctrl_i/acct_ctrl_i/we_flag. Policy registers should not be writable without proper authorization or after lock.
obligations.subject: AXI software-visible requester
obligations.object: acc_ctrl_o/acc_ctrl access-control policy bits controlling peripheral access by priv_lvl_i
obligations.expected_guard: updates to access-control policy should require authorized privilege/account-control mediation and should be blocked by relevant reglk_ctrl lock bits once locked
obligations.candidate_path: acct_wrapper AXI write path ariane_axi -> decoded en/we/address/wdata -> access-control register/state -> acc_ctrl_o -> acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] gating in riscv_peripherals
required_evidence_slots: downstream use site such as acc_ctrl_c and rom_req
knowledge_used: Access-control policy is a protected asset; configuration changes need privilege/lock mediation.
Do not assume a lock bit is effective; inspect its actual use site.
obligations.uncertainty: Need full wrapper body around lines 40-147 and top-level connections to confirm guard coverage and reset defaults.

obligations.reason: Visible assignment gates rom_req with acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0], but the scope includes multiple bootrom instances and a boot select input. Verify all ROM read paths and response valid/data paths are covered by the access-control guard and correct privilege index.
obligations.subject: NoC/AXI requester reaching boot ROM interface
obligations.object: boot ROM read data rom_rdata from bootrom/bootrom_linux
obligations.expected_guard: boot ROM read/execute access should be gated by the correct requester privilege/access-control bit and should not be bypassed by alternate ROM request/data paths or boot selection muxes
obligations.candidate_path: riscv_peripherals incoming NoC/AXI request for boot ROM -> rom_req_acct/rom_addr -> rom_req = rom_req_acct && acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][0] -> bootrom instances/read data mux
state_conditions_to_check: acc_ctrl_c construction including special j==5 alias
read response when guard is false
required_evidence_slots: response data path and mux behavior
knowledge_used: CSR/MMIO and memory-region access require requester, address decode, read enable, protected object, and expected privilege or policy guard.
Fabric/address map paths must preserve security attributes across paths.
obligations.uncertainty: Only selected assignments are visible; inspect surrounding request/response routing around lines 488-534 and address decode around line 811.

obligations.reason: rst_wrapper exports many reset outputs and accepts AXI, reglk_ctrl_i, acct_ctrl_i, and debug_mode_i. Unauthorized reset can clear security state or disrupt protected peripherals; verify reset commands are guarded and cannot bypass locks via debug mode.
obligations.subject: AXI software-visible requester and debug-mode-controlled path
obligations.object: peripheral reset control outputs rst_1..rst_13 and rst_mem/start/rst_id state
obligations.expected_guard: software-triggered peripheral resets should require privilege/access-control authorization and lock mediation; debug-mode override should be intentionally constrained
obligations.candidate_path: rst_wrapper AXI write path -> decoded start/rst_id/rst_mem -> rst_1..rst_13 outputs -> peripheral reset inputs in riscv_peripherals/wrappers
required_evidence_slots: protected object reset outputs and downstream peripheral state
knowledge_used: Reset/lifecycle controls are protected assets; mode transitions and reset operations must be mediated.
Lock bits must protect all control paths, including reset-trigger paths.
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists ports/signals but not complete control logic; inspect lines 52-181 and top-level reset wiring around lines 902-913.

obligations.reason: The top-level contains fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata signals, suggesting a readable fuse region. Need to verify requester mediation and whether fuse data can be routed to external NoC/debug outputs without proper guard.
obligations.subject: NoC/AXI/debug requester reaching fuse interface
obligations.expected_guard: fuse reads should be restricted by lifecycle/privilege/access-control or debug authorization, and fuse data should not be returned to unprivileged/debug/test response paths unless explicitly allowed
obligations.candidate_path: riscv_peripherals fuse request path -> fuse_req/fuse_addr/fuse_rdata -> any NoC/debug/AXI response or read mux
required_evidence_slots: response data path to external interface
knowledge_used: Fuses are protected assets; exposure through read muxes/debug paths is a permission vulnerability if externally observable.
Internal secret state alone is not enough; show an exposure path.
obligations.uncertainty: Only signal declarations are in fact layer; inspect around line 1613 and any read mux/NoC response routing.

obligations.reason: axi_lite_interface exposes generic AXI requests as en/we/address/data to CLINT state. Timer and IPI controls can affect privilege-level execution and should be mediated by privilege/access policy either in this interface or upstream routing.
obligations.subject: AXI/NoC requester to CLINT
obligations.operation: write timer/IPI control or read CLINT state
obligations.object: CLINT timer/IPI control state and interrupt outputs timer_irq_o/ipi_o
obligations.expected_guard: writes to timer compare/software interrupt registers should be restricted to authorized privilege/access-control policy or upstream wrapper mediation; address decode should prevent unintended register access
obligations.candidate_path: CLINT AXI-lite interface ariane_axi -> address_o/en_o/we_o/data_o -> timer/IPI registers -> timer_irq_o/ipi_o
state_conditions_to_check: acc_ctrl_c bit for CLINT if any
knowledge_used: MMIO access control requires requester, address decode, write enable, protected register, and privilege or policy guard.
Fabric/address-map checks should preserve security attributes across wrappers.
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer for axi_lite_interface is generic and lacks top-level CLINT routing details; inspect full file and riscv_peripherals CLINT connections.

obligations.reason: The top-level declares many security-sensitive peripheral NoC ports and central reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl policy signals. Need a coverage check that each sensitive peripheral access path uses the policy guard consistently, not only boot ROM.
obligations.subject: NoC/AXI requesters to top-level peripheral wrappers
obligations.object: protected peripheral NoC/MMIO request enables for AES/RSA/RNG/SHA/HMAC/debug/reset/DMA/reglk/acct and policy-controlled access paths
obligations.expected_guard: every protected peripheral request path should be gated by the intended acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] policy and, for configuration writes, by corresponding reglk_ctrl lock bits
obligations.candidate_path: reglk_ctrl/acc_ctrl policy propagation in riscv_peripherals -> acc_ctrl_c indexing by priv_lvl_i and peripheral index -> per-peripheral request enables for crypto, RNG, debug, reset, DMA, packet, account, reglk
state_conditions_to_check: acc_ctrl_c construction and peripheral index mapping
required_evidence_slots: actual request enable/data mux use site
knowledge_used: Fabric/wrapper connections may fail to preserve security attributes across paths.
A lock or access-control bit must be checked at the actual use site for each protected operation.
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer is truncated for many assignments; inspect complete riscv_peripherals for all peripheral request/valid/ready/data paths.
